- Enable ruff C901 rule (max-complexity=10) in pyproject.toml to
  enforce cyclomatic complexity as stated in constitution

  refactor (handle_authorization, post_with_retry)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

Docs: amend constitution to v2.0.0 (principle restructure)

Restructure principles to match turnovercal constitution format:
- Expand Code Quality with docstring, type annotation, complexity reqs
- Split TDD into dedicated Test-Driven Development and Phased
  Development principles
- Merge Pre-Commit Integrity into Atomic Commits & Compliance
- Resolve all TODOs: fill UX rules with captive-portal specifics,
  add concrete performance baselines from spec
- Add captive-portal constraints (Argon2, HA add-on, audit logging)

Feature/collision resolution (#11)

- Implement integration tests for the organize command, covering various scenarios including file organization by type, dry-run mode, manifest generation, naming conventions, and error handling.
- Implement integration tests for the scan command, verifying functionality for scanning directories, nested structures, file type filtering, and error handling.

fix(scan): enhance config loading mechanism

fix(manifest): add backward compatibility for manifest structure

- Update the manifest generator to include an operations property as an alias for entries for backward compatibility.

fix(operation): prevent execution of operations when no operations are present

- Add a critical early return in the operation executor to avoid executing performOperation when there are no operations to perform.

commit.message:
SCRUM-79: Fix Commit Messages (#26)

* revamped rust module prototype

* Checkpoint 2 for commit msg refactor. Consider pipeline.md for restructure

* Phase 2 complete of plan.md

* refactor(desktop): migrate all ALS interpretation to Rust NAPI parser

- Add diff_from_snapshot() export: diffs committed snapshot JSON against
  current ALS file, skipping HEAD blob download on the fast path
- Add generateCommitMessage() export: ports buildCommitMessage() logic
  from JS to Rust, removing the last JS function that read DiffReport data
- Convert all NAPI exports to async via tokio::task::spawn_blocking,
  keeping the Electron main process unblocked during ALS parsing
- Add schema_version field to Project struct with serde default for
  backwards-compatible snapshot deserialization
- Rewrite project.ts from ~800 lines to 61: remove all fast-xml-parser-
  based XML parsing, decompression, structuralCompareAls, buildLocalDiffFromAls,
  getAlsContent, and related helpers; retain only gitBin + git shell commands
- Replace get-als-content and no-commits fallback handlers with parseAls
- Remove buildCommitMessage() from main.ts
- Delete 9 orphaned types from types/index.ts (DecompressedAls, AlsChange,
  StructuralCompareResult, AudioTrackEntry, MidiTrackEntry, etc.)
- Uninstall fast-xml-parser (removes 68 packages)

JS is now purely transport: paths and buffers only. All ALS semantics
live in the Rust crate. One parser to update if Ableton's format changes.

* chore(desktop): remove old dist/vendor parser, restore git binary

* Fix build and ordering issue by implementing sorting the hashmap

* Remove outdated PIPELINE Claude file.

* Update apps/desktop/native/semantic-diff/src/diff.rs

Fix issue of unexpected characters when overflowing integers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update "children" model for the als structure to use a default so it doesn't fail in the event of empty children

commit.message:
refactor: codebase quality consolidation — DRY, dead code removal, YAGNI cleanup (#374)

* refactor(lexer): deduplicate keyword table into single source of truth

The keyword-to-string mapping was maintained in three places:
Display::fmt, keyword_str(), and ALL_KEYWORDS. Adding a keyword
required editing all three.

A define_keywords! macro now holds the single canonical table
and generates keyword_str(), is_keyword(), and ALL_KEYWORDS.
Display::fmt delegates to keyword_str() for keywords, removing
its own keyword match arms entirely.

Net reduction of ~130 lines with no behaviour change.

* refactor(types): add Default impl for FnSig to eliminate construction boilerplate

FnSig was constructed at 25+ sites in check.rs with the same defaults
repeated verbatim (empty type_params, empty bounds, false for async/pure/
kwargs, None for doc_comment). Adding a manual Default impl (Ty::Unit as
return_type default) enables struct update syntax at every call site.

Also removes a now-unfulfilled #[expect(clippy::too_many_lines)] on
register_machine_decl — the boilerplate reduction brought it under the
threshold.

* refactor(types): remove dead helper APIs

Verify the targeted TypeError constructors and ModuleRegistry predicates
have no production callers, then delete them instead of carrying test-only
surface area.

Keep the remaining error-formatting tests focused on observable behaviour by
constructing TypeError values directly, and drop the module-registry self-tests
that only existed to exercise the removed helpers.

* refactor(runtime): share lossy C string decoding

The WASM bridge had a private helper for decoding NUL-terminated C strings
into owned Rust strings. Reusing hew-cabi's existing cstr_to_str helper would
have changed behaviour because that helper rejects invalid UTF-8 instead of
performing the bridge's existing lossy conversion.

Add a lossy owned-string helper to hew-cabi and switch bridge metadata loading
to use it so the conversion logic lives in one place without adding a new crate
edge or changing null/invalid-UTF-8 handling.

* refactor: deduplicate wasm analysis setup

Extract a single helper for the repeated hew-wasm parse and type-check scaffold so hover, completions, signature help, inlay hints, and diagnostics all share the same setup.

This keeps the behaviour unchanged while removing duplicate checker construction and the repeated parse-success gating logic.

* refactor(cli): remove dead command surface

Remove the no-op hew --Werror flag and its unused compile option so the compiler help, completions, and spec match the implemented behaviour.

Drop adze init --bin and the placeholder adze namespace list command so the advertised CLI only includes working paths. Add a regression test to ensure removed build-style flags fail with a clear unknown-option error.

* refactor(cli): deduplicate gitignore updates

adze init and install both needed the same .gitignore append semantics,
but install had drift-prone duplicate logic in a second helper.

Route install through ensure_gitignore_entry(), delete the duplicate helper,
and retarget the focused tests at the shared path. Add coverage for trimmed
existing lines and files without a trailing newline so the shared helper's
boundary behaviour stays locked in.

* refactor(cli): deduplicate hew binary lookup

Both the REPL and the test runner used the same current_exe()-based search for the built hew binary, but each kept its own copy of that logic.

Move the search into a shared utility module so path resolution behaviour stays aligned across subprocess-based CLI features and future fixes only need one change.

* refactor(runtime): deduplicate profiler snapshot JSON

Extract small JSON helpers for profiler snapshot endpoints so routing, connection, and cluster snapshots share the same array assembly logic without introducing a heavier serialization layer.

This keeps field-specific formatting in each subsystem, adds proper JSON escaping for cluster member addresses, and adds focused snapshot contract tests for the refactored endpoints.

* refactor(parser): share string escape decoding

Deduplicate the parser's plain-string and interpolated-string
escape tables by routing both paths through one helper.

Keep interpolation parsing separate by only parameterizing the
extra escaped delimiters that must stay literal inside
interpolated strings.

* test(runtime): isolate shutdown stateful tests

The Phase 8 cherry-picks passed individually, but the consolidation branch still
failed under cargo test --workspace because shutdown tests were mutating shared
global phase and supervisor state in parallel.

Serialize the shutdown test module with a test-only mutex and reset helper so
each test starts from a clean runtime state. Keep the fix local to the existing
test module, and include the rustfmt changes needed for the profiler snapshot
assertions so cargo fmt --all -- --check stays green.

* docs(serialize): sync boundary schema comments

Keep the Rust MessagePack boundary docs aligned with the fields that
TypedProgram actually serializes so the embedded C++ reader contract is
clear at the source of truth.

Also update the C++ Program schema_version comment to match the current
reader behaviour, which requires an explicit exact schema version rather
than accepting pre-versioned payloads.

* refactor(serialize): deduplicate TypedProgram wrappers

Both top-level serialization paths carried the same field list and setup code,
with only the module graph representation varying between MessagePack and
JSON. That duplication made schema changes easy to miss in one path.

Replace the parallel wrappers with a generic TypedProgram constructor and keep
ModuleGraphJson as the only JSON-specific adapter. This preserves the wire
shape while giving both serializers one place to build the shared payload.

* refactor(types): deduplicate actor field binding

Extract a shared Checker helper for binding actor fields into scope so
actor methods, init blocks, terminate blocks, and receive handlers all
follow the same path.

Add focused typecheck coverage for actor methods and terminate blocks so
all four binding entry points are exercised.

* refactor(parser): share wire field parsing

Deduplicate wire field number and modifier parsing between
different numbering semantics in the caller paths.

Also share outer json/yaml naming-case extraction and add focused
parser tests covering both code paths.

* refactor(runtime): remove unused load_ca_cert placeholder

Drop the unused QUIC CA loader and the unit test that existed only to exercise the placeholder. This keeps the runtime API and documentation aligned with the QUIC transport features that are actually implemented today.

* refactor: remove verified dead code across workspace

Drop parser and type helper accessors that no longer have any in-tree callers.
Keep the lone #[allow(dead_code)] on the WASM actor stub because it remains an intentional platform split, and leave feature flags and imports unchanged because the sweep did not verify any other dead code.

* refactor(cabi): inline single-impl SinkBacking trait

The shared C ABI no longer exposes SinkBacking as a public trait.
HewSink now owns sink behaviour through constructor helpers, with a
callback-based path for exact-item native sinks and a Write-based helper
for ordinary writers.

This keeps custom sink semantics available to native packages while
removing the bespoke public trait and simplifying runtime and HTTP
call sites.

* refactor(runtime): remove dead export metadata tooling

Remove the unused stdlib export-metadata generator and the proc-macro\npath that only fed it.\n\nhew-types now loads canonical stdlib .hew sources directly, and neither\nthe Makefile nor CI references the old generator anymore. This drops the\ndead workspace members and strips the now-unused export-meta feature\nfrom hew-runtime and the affected stdlib crates.\n\nBREAKING CHANGE: remove hew-stdlib-gen, hew-export-macro,\nhew-export-types, and the export-meta Cargo feature from the affected\ncrates.\n\nCo-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(types): extract shared module prefix stripping

The lookup_type_def, lookup_type_def_mut, and lookup_fn_sig methods
all duplicated the same module-qualified name resolution logic.
Extract strip_module_prefix() helper and simplify all three callers.

refactor: two-level workload→feature hierarchy with presets

Restructure the data model from flat workloads to a workload→feature
hierarchy enabling both bulk workload toggling and individual feature
control.

Data model changes:
- Add Feature struct (id, name, plugins, config_lua, default_enabled)
- Add Preset struct (id, name, description, workloads)
- Workload.features replaces direct plugins/config_lua fields
- Backward compat: old plugins/config_lua auto-migrated via normalize()

Manifest changes:
- Rename InstanceManifest.features to .workloads (serde alias for compat)
- Add disabled_features and extra_features for fine-grained overrides

Init.lua generation:
- generate_init_lua_full() iterates features within workloads
- Respects disabled_features/extra_features overrides
- Wrapper generate_init_lua() preserves existing call sites

TUI changes:
- Hierarchical WorkloadCheckbox model with expand/collapse
- Space toggles workload (all features), →/l expands, ←/h collapses
- Individual feature toggling within expanded workloads

CLI changes:
- @preset syntax in --enable (e.g. @ide-core, @ide-full)
- parse_features() resolves presets to workload lists

Presets: minimal (base only), ide-core (LSP+tree+tabs), ide-full (all)

feat: multi-model review panel with consolidated scoring

Implements a "jury" review system where multiple LLM models independently
review generated code, then a consolidator synthesizes consensus scores.

Architecture:
- Config: reviewer_models: [list] replaces reviewer_model (backward compat kept)
- First model in list acts as the consolidator
- All reviewers run in parallel (goroutines with separate Copilot clients)
- Consolidator receives all individual reviews and produces consensus
- Fallback: if consolidation fails, uses median scores

Changes:
- config.go: Add ReviewerModels field + EffectiveReviewerModels() method
- config.go: Updated validation (no model overlap, no duplicates)
- reviewer.go: Add PanelReviewer with ReviewPanel() and consolidation
- types.go: Add Model field to ReviewResult, ReviewPanel to EvalReport
- engine.go: Wire panel reviewer, fix duration to track generation only
- html.go: Add review panel table showing per-reviewer + consensus scores
- main.go: Wire panel creation when reviewer_models has 2+ entries
- All configs updated with reviewer_models: [claude-opus-4.6, gpt-4.1]

commit.message:
feat(backends): Wire torsion energy in JAX engines

Add atan2-based signed dihedral torsion energy to JaxEngine, replacing
the NotImplementedError placeholder. Update torsion matching in both
JaxEngine and JaxMDEngine to use forcefield.match_torsion() with
element-based fallback instead of the previous ff_row-only matching.

JaxMDEngine already had torsion energy computation implemented but
the matching never produced results due to the restrictive ff_row
guard. Both engines now pass all torsion contract tests including
a new cross-engine parity test against OpenMM.

Review fixes:
- Fix dihedral sign convention (b0 = i→j, matching IUPAC/OpenMM)
- Remove incorrect improper torsion assignment in OpenMM (proper
  improper detection requires trigonal-centre identification)
- Add is_improper field to _TorsionTerm for future use
- Pass is_improper in update_forcefield() torsion rematching
- Add cross-engine parity test (JAX/JAX-MD vs OpenMM)
